threats.title: UNIv3 TVL Overcounting via Aggregate Pool Liquidity
threats.hypothesis_description: The UNIv3Connector calculates TVL using the aggregate liquidity of all NFTs in the same tick range managed by the position manager, rather than the specific vault's NFT liquidity.
threats.investigation_rationale: If the TVL includes other users' liquidity, the vault's share price will be artificially inflated, allowing value extraction.

proof_of_signal.pattern: pool.positions(key) returns aggregate liquidity for the positionManager, not the specific tokenId

preconditions: Vault has an active UNIv3 position
Attacker has capital to provide liquidity to the same pool and tick range

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker adds liquidity via the same positionManager at the same ticks
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker inflates TVL, then withdraws vault shares at the inflated price to steal funds

economic_questions: Does the positionManager aggregate liquidity by owner and tick range?
Can an attacker atomically add liquidity, trigger a vault withdrawal, and remove liquidity?
validation_requirements: Verify Uniswap V3 pool.positions behavior
Calculate profit margin considering UNIv3 swap fees and gas costs

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_execution_cost: Gas for UNIv3 mint, vault withdraw, UNIv3 burn
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Proportional to the ratio of attacker liquidity to vault liquidity
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: High, as it directly extracts vault principal

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Sufficient capital to meaningfully inflate the tick range liquidity
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Any time the vault has a UNIv3 position
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: No price impact required, only liquidity addition

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Loss of principal proportional to TVL inflation

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: UNIv3 tick ranges and position manager behavior
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Compare pool.positions liquidity vs positionManager.positions(tokenId) liquidity

threats.title: Camelot LP Pricing Vulnerable to Flash Loan Manipulation
threats.hypothesis_description: CamelotConnector values LP tokens using the sum of pool reserves multiplied by oracle prices. This formula is mathematically vulnerable to manipulation by imbalancing the pool.
threats.investigation_rationale: Due to the constant product invariant, imbalancing a pool increases the total value of its reserves when priced by an external oracle, allowing TVL inflation.

proof_of_signal.pattern: balanceThis * (_getValue(tokenA, base, reserves0) + _getValue(tokenB, base, reserves1)) / totalSupply

preconditions: Vault holds Camelot LP tokens
Flash loan liquidity available for pool tokens

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Linear sum of reserves valued at oracle prices
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Flash loan swap to heavily imbalance the Camelot pool
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Imbalanced pool increases calculated TVL, allowing attacker to withdraw shares at an inflated exchange rate

economic_questions: How much does the reserve value increase for a given swap size?
Do swap fees outweigh the TVL inflation profit?
validation_requirements: Model the AMM invariant against the linear pricing formula
Calculate exact profit margins including flash loan and swap fees

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Large flash loan to significantly imbalance the pool
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_execution_cost: Flash loan fees + Camelot swap fees
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: High, depends on pool depth and vault LP holdings
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Requires deep imbalance to overcome swap fees

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: High flash loan liquidity relative to Camelot pool size
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When vault has large Camelot LP position
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Temporary extreme price impact on Camelot pool

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Value of the Camelot LP position

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Simulate reserve changes during large swaps

threats.title: UNIv3 TVL Vulnerable to Spot Price Manipulation
threats.hypothesis_description: UNIv3Connector uses the pool's current spot price (slot0) to calculate the underlying token amounts for its liquidity, which are then valued by the oracle.
threats.investigation_rationale: Spot prices in AMMs can be easily manipulated within a single block using flash loans, altering the calculated token amounts and inflating TVL.
preconditions: Vault holds UNIv3 liquidity
Flash loan liquidity available to move UNIv3 spot price

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Flash loan swap to move sqrtPriceX96
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Moving spot price changes amount0/amount1 ratio. If oracle values them differently, total TVL inflates, allowing profitable withdrawal.

economic_questions: Which direction of price movement maximizes TVL?
Is the TVL inflation sufficient to cover swap fees?
validation_requirements: Model UNIv3 liquidity math against oracle pricing
Calculate profit margins including flash loan and swap fees

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Large flash loan to move UNIv3 spot price
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_execution_cost: Flash loan fees + UNIv3 swap fees
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Depends on liquidity concentration and oracle price divergence

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Temporary extreme price impact on UNIv3 pool

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Liquidity tick ranges and pool fee tier
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Simulate amount0/amount1 changes with spot price movement

threats.title: Dolomite Borrow Position Debt Excluded from TVL
threats.hypothesis_description: When opening a borrow position in DolomiteConnector, the position is incorrectly removed from the registry instead of being added, causing the debt to be excluded from TVL calculations.
threats.investigation_rationale: If debt is not tracked, the vault's net TVL is artificially inflated, leading to incorrect share pricing and potential value extraction.

proof_of_signal.pattern: registry.updateHoldingPosition(..., true) called when opening position

preconditions: Keeper opens a borrow position in Dolomite

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: removePosition flag set to true during openBorrowPosition
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Normal protocol operation results in untracked debt
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Users can withdraw shares at an inflated exchange rate because the debt is not subtracted from the vault's assets.

economic_questions: How much debt can be hidden this way?
Does the missing position ID cause other accounting failures?
validation_requirements: Verify registry state after openBorrowPosition
Check if TVLHelper iterates over the missing position

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Equal to the untracked debt amount
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: 100% of the untracked debt distributed among withdrawing users
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Multi-block (wait for keeper to borrow, then withdraw)

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Immediately after keeper opens a Dolomite borrow position

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Up to the total borrowed amount in Dolomite

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify TVL calculation excludes the removed position

threats.title: Holding Position Index Corruption via Swap-and-Pop Key Mismatch
threats.hypothesis_description: The Registry uses different keys for adding and removing holding positions. Adding uses msg.sender, while swap-and-pop removal uses calculatorConnector. For token positions, these differ, corrupting the index mapping.
threats.investigation_rationale: Corrupted indices prevent positions from being updated or removed, permanently locking them in the registry and potentially causing a DoS when the maximum position limit is reached.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Key creation uses msg.sender, swap update uses holdingPositions[positionIndex].calculatorConnector

preconditions: Vault has multiple holding positions
A token position (type 0) is moved during a swap-and-pop removal of another position

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Mismatched keccak256 keys for isPositionUsed mapping
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Normal position removal corrupts the mapping for the swapped position
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit, but causes severe economic griefing by locking vault funds and preventing rebalancing.

economic_questions: Can this be triggered intentionally to freeze a specific vault?
Does this prevent withdrawals if a position cannot be liquidated?
validation_requirements: Trace the exact state of isPositionUsed after a swap-and-pop
Verify if the corrupted position can ever be recovered

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Minimal (just enough to trigger position changes)
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_execution_cost: Gas for deposit/withdraw to trigger position removal

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: calculatorConnector vs msg.sender for token positions
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Simulate array swap and mapping update

threats.title: Vault DoS via TVL Calculation Underflow
threats.hypothesis_description: Multiple connectors calculate TVL by subtracting debt from collateral. If debt exceeds collateral (underwater position), the subtraction underflows in Solidity 0.8, reverting the entire vault TVL calculation.
threats.investigation_rationale: A single underwater position will cause TVLHelper.getTVL to revert, completely freezing all vault deposits, withdrawals, and share price updates.

proof_of_signal.pattern: tvl = totalDepositAmount - totalBAmount; without checking if totalBAmount > totalDepositAmount

preconditions: Vault has an active borrow position in a supported protocol
Position becomes underwater (debt > collateral) due to market movement or liquidation fees

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Unchecked subtraction of debt from collateral
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Market volatility or targeted oracle manipulation to force position underwater
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit, but causes severe economic griefing and prevents users from rescuing funds during market crashes.

economic_questions: Can an attacker artificially inflate the debt via flash loans to trigger this?
Is there any recovery mechanism if the TVL calculation reverts?
validation_requirements: Test TVL calculation with simulated underwater position
Check if emergency roles can bypass the TVL check to rescue funds

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Depends on cost to manipulate oracle or pool state
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Persistent until position is liquidated or market recovers

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: None if triggered by natural market crash

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Inability to withdraw funds during critical market periods

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify lack of zero-floor checks in connectors

threats.title: Stuck Native ETH in SwapAndBridgeHandler
threats.hypothesis_description: The executeSwap and executeBridge functions are payable but call their implementation contracts without forwarding msg.value, causing native ETH to be permanently stuck in the handler.
threats.investigation_rationale: Users or keepers sending native ETH to execute cross-chain bridges or swaps will lose their funds, as the implementation contract receives 0 ETH and the handler has no rescue function for non-owners.
preconditions: Caller sends native ETH to executeSwap or executeBridge

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Missing value forwarding in external call
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Normal protocol usage with native ETH results in lost funds
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit for attacker, but direct loss of funds for users/keepers.

economic_questions: Does the protocol intend to support native ETH swaps/bridges?
Can the owner rescue funds from the handler?
validation_requirements: Test executeSwap with msg.value > 0
Verify ETH balance of handler increases and implementation receives 0

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Any time native ETH is used

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Keepers or eligible users executing swaps
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All native ETH sent to the handler

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Payable modifiers and external call syntax
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Check ETH balances before and after call

threats.title: Registry Administration Bricked via Modifier Logic Bug
threats.hypothesis_description: The onlyVaultMaintainer modifier uses an OR (||) condition instead of AND (&&) in its revert check, requiring the caller to be BOTH the maintainer AND have the EMERGENCY_ROLE.
threats.investigation_rationale: This logic error prevents the maintainer from executing core administrative functions like adding connectors or updating trusted tokens, effectively bricking vault configuration.
preconditions: Maintainer attempts to call a protected function
Maintainer does not simultaneously hold the EMERGENCY_ROLE

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Incorrect boolean logic in access control
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit, but prevents protocol from adapting to market conditions or adding new yield sources.

economic_questions: Can the governor grant the EMERGENCY_ROLE to the maintainer as a workaround?
Does this prevent emergency interventions?
validation_requirements: Test onlyVaultMaintainer with maintainer address without EMERGENCY_ROLE
Verify revert occurs

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Inability to access new yield strategies

threats.title: SNXv3 TVL Inflation via Double Counting Collateral
threats.hypothesis_description: The SNXv3Connector calculates TVL by adding totalDeposited and totalAssigned. If the underlying protocol's totalDeposited already includes assigned collateral, this results in double counting.
threats.investigation_rationale: Double counting collateral permanently inflates the vault's TVL, leading to an artificially high share price that allows early depositors to extract value from new depositors.
deployment_assumptions: Synthetix V3 getAccountCollateral returns totalDeposited inclusive of totalAssigned
preconditions: Vault has an active SNXv3 position with delegated (assigned) collateral

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Keeper delegates collateral, instantly inflating TVL
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Existing depositors can withdraw shares at the inflated TVL, effectively stealing from the vault's actual assets.

economic_questions: Does SNX V3 totalDeposited include totalAssigned?
How much can the TVL be inflated?
validation_requirements: Verify SNX V3 CoreProxy getAccountCollateral return values
Calculate exact TVL inflation percentage

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Proportional to the amount of assigned collateral

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: After keeper delegates collateral in SNX

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Loss of principal due to inflated entry price
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Value of the assigned SNX collateral

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Check if totalDeposited >= totalAssigned always holds

threats.title: Disabled Swap/Bridge Routes Bypass
threats.hypothesis_description: The onlyExistingRoute modifier uses an AND (&&) condition, meaning a route is only rejected if its address is zero AND it is disabled. A disabled route with a non-zero address remains usable.
threats.investigation_rationale: If a route is disabled due to a vulnerability or compromise, keepers or eligible users can still execute transactions through it, defeating the emergency disable mechanism.
preconditions: A route is disabled by the maintainer (isEnabled = false)
The route's address is not set to address(0)

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Caller uses a disabled route ID in executeSwap/executeBridge
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Allows exploitation of a known vulnerable external bridge/swap contract that the protocol attempted to disable.

economic_questions: Can the maintainer set the route address to zero to fully disable it?
Are there currently any disabled routes in production?
validation_requirements: Test executeSwap with a disabled route with a non-zero address
Verify the transaction succeeds

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Depends on the vulnerability of the disabled route

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: After a route is disabled but before it is removed

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Loss of funds if routed through compromised protocol

threats.title: Cross-chain TVL Sync Reordering Arbitrage
threats.hypothesis_description: The OmnichainManagerBaseChain.updateTVL function does not enforce monotonicity of the updateTime parameter. If LayerZero messages are reordered, an older TVL value can overwrite a newer one.
threats.investigation_rationale: Reordered messages can cause the base chain to temporarily use a stale, incorrect TVL for share pricing, creating an arbitrage opportunity.

proof_of_signal.pattern: No check that updateTime > current positionTimestamp

preconditions: Vault operates across multiple chains
LayerZero message delivery is delayed or reordered

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker monitors for out-of-order TVL updates
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: If an old, lower TVL overwrites a new, higher TVL, attacker buys cheap shares. When TVL corrects, attacker withdraws for profit.

economic_questions: How frequently are TVL updates sent?
What is the typical variance in TVL between updates?
validation_requirements: Test updateTVL with an older timestamp
Verify the registry position is updated with the stale data

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Capital to deposit and buy shares

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: During periods of high network congestion causing LZ delays

threats.title: Arbitrary External Calls via Flash Loan Callback
threats.hypothesis_description: The BalancerFlashLoan.receiveFlashLoan function executes arbitrary calls to destinationConnector addresses without validating that they are trusted connectors in the registry.
threats.investigation_rationale: While this requires the keeper to initiate the flash loan, a compromised keeper or malicious strategy could use this to bypass connector restrictions and execute arbitrary transactions with the flash loaned funds.

proof_of_signal.pattern: destinationConnector[i].call{ value: 0, gas: gas[i] }(callingData[i]); without registry validation

preconditions: Keeper multisig is compromised or executes a malicious strategy

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Missing isAddressTrusted check for destinationConnector array
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Keeper passes malicious contract addresses in destinationConnector
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker drains flash loaned funds or manipulates external protocols using the vault's identity.

economic_questions: Can the flash loan be used to approve tokens to an attacker?
Does the vault identity provide special privileges in external protocols?
validation_requirements: Test receiveFlashLoan with an untrusted destinationConnector
Verify the call succeeds

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Up to the flash loan amount or vault's external privileges

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Loss of funds if flash loan repayment fails and pulls from vault
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Vault assets accessible to the flash loan contract

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Check token flow and approvals during callback
